raw_response: {"rating": -1.4, "lean": "Lean Left", "explanation": "This is a news-style fact-check. It leans Left because the author's own prose characterizes the White House’s methodology as cherry-picking and adds comparative framing that diminishes Trump’s claim by highlighting Biden-era gains. Balanced sourcing tempers the tilt, so the rating is a modest Lean Left. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author asserts that the White House \"cherry-picked data that favours a higher earnings gain,\" a loaded judgment targeting a Republican administration; (2) The piece includes an editorial conclusion that the added comparison to a prior administration \"undercuts the notion that Trump’s gains have been unusually high,\" a framing choice that disadvantages Trump; (3) Structural emphasis consistently spotlights limitations of the White House’s preferred measure (exclusion of part-time workers, lack of inflation adjustment) without discussing potential merits of that metric, cumulatively steering readers toward the counter-narrative; (4) Mitigating factor: the article cites both a center-right and a liberal economist and grounds its analysis in BLS datasets, which reduces the overall bias magnitude."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news-style fact-check. It leans Left because the author's own prose characterizes the White House’s methodology as cherry-picking and adds comparative framing that diminishes Trump’s claim by highlighting Biden-era gains. Balanced sourcing tempers the tilt, so the rating is a modest Lean Left. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author asserts that the White House "cherry-picked data that favours a higher earnings gain," a loaded judgment targeting a Republican administration; (2) The piece includes an editorial conclusion that the added comparison to a prior administration "undercuts the notion that Trump’s gains have been unusually high," a framing choice that disadvantages Trump; (3) Structural emphasis consistently spotlights limitations of the White House’s preferred measure (exclusion of part-time workers, lack of inflation adjustment) without discussing potential merits of that metric, cumulatively steering readers toward the counter-narrative; (4) Mitigating factor: the article cites both a center-right and a liberal economist and grounds its analysis in BLS datasets, which reduces the overall bias magnitude.

explanation: This is a news-style fact-check. It leans Left because the author's own prose characterizes the White House’s methodology as cherry-picking and adds comparative framing that diminishes Trump’s claim by highlighting Biden-era gains. Balanced sourcing tempers the tilt, so the rating is a modest Lean Left. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author asserts that the White House "cherry-picked data that favours a higher earnings gain," a loaded judgment targeting a Republican administration; (2) The piece includes an editorial conclusion that the added comparison to a prior administration "undercuts the notion that Trump’s gains have been unusually high," a framing choice that disadvantages Trump; (3) Structural emphasis consistently spotlights limitations of the White House’s preferred measure (exclusion of part-time workers, lack of inflation adjustment) without discussing potential merits of that metric, cumulatively steering readers toward the counter-narrative; (4) Mitigating factor: the article cites both a center-right and a liberal economist and grounds its analysis in BLS datasets, which reduces the overall bias magnitude.
